Name:

(2011 - 2013)
Notes: Era begins (roughly) after the release of the deluxe version of 'Cruis'n USA' in July 2011, and ends when the project was released on May 21, 2013. 'Owl Pharaoh' missed multiple release dates and was reworked several times as Travis's industry connections grew (see the Tracklists section for more details).

The instrumental appears to be a reworked version of the FKi-produced beat used for "Conclusion" with Machine Gun Kelly and Travis Porter. Name:

[prod. Young Chop & MIKE DEAN]
(AKA: Blocka)
Notes: Near-complete; some of the vocal effects in the background are less noticeable, and it doesn't feature the background vocals found on the final version at around 2:52. Leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman.

Name:
🏆 Can't Look In My Eyes [V1]
(feat. Kid Cudi & Kanye West)
[prod. Anthony Kilhoffer, Kanye West, MIKE DEAN & Travis Scott]
(AKA: Fuck)
Notes: Song that appears on the August 2012 tracklist; production credits confirmed through tweets from Travis (Mike Dean was mentioned in a separate tweet). Posts from some KTT users and GOODMUSICINSIDER indicate that it is an early version of Kid Cudi's "Too Bad I Have to Destroy You Now" (previously/alternatively known as "Can't Look In My Eyes"), before Travis and Kanye were cut. Pharaoxx stated that "Fuck" was actually "Shit On You"; the evidence seems to suggest that he was wrong, but it's possible that both things are true, with "Fuck" developing into "Can't Look In My Eyes", and either the instrumental or Travis's vocals being used in some capacity on either "Bad Mood" (due to the heavy presence of the word "fuck") or "Shit On You".

Name:
Kid Cudi - Can't Look In My Eyes [V2]
(feat. Kanye West & Travis Scott)
[prod. Kid Cudi & Dot da Genius]
Notes: Later version of "Fuck"; eventually became "Too Bad I Have To Destroy You Now". A snippet circulated on KTT in April 2013, but it appears to be lost. At a 2014 meet-and-greet, according to a KTT user, Travis expressed mild annoyance when "TBIHTDYN" was played over the speakers, stating: "Aye, turn this shit off. Me and 'Ye were supposed to be on this but [Cudi] took us off". According to another KTT user, Travis confirmed that Cudi had already reworked the beat by this point, likely bringing in the "Son of Flynn" sample.

Name:
Can't Look In My Eyes [V3]
(feat. Kanye West & Kid Cudi)
[prod. Kid Cudi & Dot da Genius]
Notes: Travis tweeted on March 26, 2013, that he wanted to drop "Can't Look In My Eyes" after Cudi cut it from his album, and he stated on Twitter that the original version was better after it released, which was probably in reference to "Fuck". This is likely the Kanye and Cudi song that Pharaohxx mentioned would appear on the iTunes deluxe version of "Owl Pharaoh", but the project never released and Cudi took the song back anyway. Leaked audio clips from an unknown time show what sounds like will.i.am calling the song "fresh" during a listening session of 'Owl Pharaoh'.

[prod. WondaGurl]

YoungxSho noted in several KTT posts that he was a friend of Travis and had leaked a couple other things in the past, so it's possibly just a leak that he wanted some clout for; he claimed to have made it, but he would've needed access to the stems for it so it's likely legitimate. Travis himself tweeted that he was going to re-release the song after being unhappy with the mix that made the XXL Freshman tape, but he never did - this could be the version that he wanted to drop.

Said by Travis to be for 'Owl Pharaoh' during his 2013 SXSW set and XXL Freshman interview, and Pharaohxx later implied that it would be on the iTunes deluxe edition, but it never saw a release, most likely due to the possible legal issues surrounding it. The instrumental leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman; the actual track leaked in a four-pack on May 16, 2016, which pissed Travis off.

Uses the same instrumental sample as Trouble's "Molly World", featuring Future; most likely coincidence and not a sample. This unmastered version leaked November 5, 2021.

Notes:
OG Filename: 01 QUINTANA MIGOS REMIX
Early version featuring the same intro as the original song, minus Travis's vocals, and has an open verse, presumably for Offset, who was arrested before he could record for it. Leaked December 19, 2022.

Name:
Quintana (Remix) [Demo 2]
(feat. Quavo & Takeoff) 
[prod. Sak Pase, Travis Scott & Anthony Kilhoffer]
Notes:
OG Filename: Quintana Remix 2.0

Name:
⭐ Sin City [Demo 1] 
(feat. Diana Gordon) 
[prod. TBHits & Travis Scott]
Notes: Very early version of "Sin City" with Diana Gordon (f.k.a. Wynter Gordon) on the chorus and an extra verse from Travis, originally intended for an earlier version of 'Owl Pharaoh' before Kanye took it for 'Cruel Summer' (it doesn't appear on any known tracklists, but now-deleted tweets from Travis essentially confirmed that it was intended for it at some stage). A low-quality version was leaked on KTT on June 1, 2013, by someone who worked with Tony Loney (and presumably Travis). Pharaohxx claimed to have it in HQ, but he purposefully left it out of the pack of demos that he sold to KTT user Fitz, as he anticipated that it would leak.

Name:
Smith & Western
[prod. Portishead & Travis Scott]
Notes: Very early song from 2011 that appears on a few tracklists. An instrumental that's essentially a loop of "Biscuit" by Portishead leaked on July 19, 2013, after being acquired from Tony Loney by KTT users Carlos and xxman. Part of this instrumental was previously used in an ILLROOTS promotion and was known as "iLLworld" on KTT; the audio is gone, but the original article from January 25, 2012, can still be accessed, with Travis being credited for the music.

Name:
⭐ Time Machine
[prod. MIKE DEAN & Travis Scott]
Notes: Leaked by Fitz on KTT on June 1, 2013, after he was sold a pack of early 'Owl Pharaoh' tracks by Pharaohxx; can be seen on the August 2012 tracklist.

Name:
✨ Trap In Mexico [V1]
(with Trae Tha Truth)
Notes: Likely made sometime in early - mid-2012 and, according to Pharaohxx, serves as an early concept for "Quintana". KTT user Lord Elm leaked snippets of the song in early 2013, but they are likely lost. Likely the same version that was 'released' by DJ S.R. on January 27, 2016.

Name:
travis1
[prod. Travis Scott]

"Around Me" was a name given to it by KTT members after it leaked; the file was titled "Travis x Chip", though this is likely not the OG filename.

Untitled collaboration likely recorded after the two met for the XXL Freshman cover; possibly the song that was rumoured on KTT to be dropping in July 2013 (unclear where this rumour originated). Leaked July 6, 2021. The file was leaked with a fake date at the end, which was added by a scammer.

Name:
Upper Echelon [Demo 1]

Version with a completed second verse and additional production from Mike Dean; also features minor additional vocals from T.I. Performed as part of Travis's main SXSW 2013 set, as well as during the MTV Woodies Showcase at the same event. Clearly intended as a performance version, but it remains listed as a demo due to the new vocals. Leaked December 25, 2022. Censored until the new verse starts; the last hook is uncensored.

Teased by Travis on April 29, 2013, via Vine; another (deleted) Vine posted by Tony Loney showed that TNGHT were in the studio at the time, likely meaning they produced it. A Kanye West feature was rumoured because of a tweet from Travis, but the tweet happened before the remix was previewed; Drake was rumoured as Travis was at his house the night before the snippet was posted, but this was pure speculation, and Travis later said (possibly sometime in 2014) that they hadn't worked together.

Name: Owl Pharaoh [Early Cover Art]
Notes: An early cover art, featuring thirteen owls in the shape of a circumflex. This art was posted to Travis's Tumblr in 2012, and was embedded in the "16 Chapels" file posted to his website. This file also had the album metadata as 'Owl Pharaoh', so it's safe to assume that it was the cover art for a time.

Name: Owl Pharaoh [iTunes Cover Art]
Notes: Picture posted to Instagram shortly before the release of the free version of the project. While no primary source is available, when KTT user Carlos 'revealed' this information, multiple other KTT users stated that another source had already told them. Should probably be in the unconfirmed section for now.

Name: Untitled Album [Project | V1]
Notes: While it wasn't officially announced until later, Travis was openly working on a studio album alongside 'Owl Pharaoh'; Pharaohxx said that was originally meant to release in September 2013. Travis stated that the instrumental for JAY-Z's "Somewhereinamerica" was intended for the project, and he also said that he wanted to put "Can't Look In My Eyes" on it after Cudi initially scrapped it. According to Pharaohxx, "Armageddon" was also being saved for the album. The project became Travis's main focus after the iTunes deluxe edition of 'Owl Pharaoh' was scrapped (see 'V2').

OG Filename: KBXX 2013 MAINTRACK TRAVISSCOTTUPPERECHELON
Early version that was sent to KBXX radio as a teaser, and so is censored. Appears slightly off-beat in certain areas and Travis's verse is missing the last four bars. Also has no features. Volume warning.

Though this version isn't really 'for' Travis, the bassline was carried across into "3500", so it fits here. Full uncut file leaked December 28, 2022.

Been rumoured for years, but Pharaohxx only mentioned that Travis produced on the song.

Notes: Presumably exists, as 'V2' and 'V3' were leaked under those respective titles, but it's unconfirmed considering how bare-bones 'V2' is.

Name:
Stadium Red
[prod. Duke Dinero & The Superiors]
Notes: Song of unconfirmed nature recorded at Stadium Red in New York, supposedly labelled as being part of 'Owl Pharaoh' at some stage. KTT user William made a post on February 24, 2015, stating that they had found the session while at the studio, claiming that it was a mumble reference with a "hard" beat. William then bounced it, and the file he posted contained adlibs from Travis, over the instrumental for what became "No Way Out" by Chinx. It now appears to be lost so it's hard to confirm its legitimacy; William's original comments about it being a mumble demo conflicts with what he leaked, but he may have only bounced the adlibs on purpose. Insider skr8up claimed in the thread that William would be facing legal action as a result, so that might make it more likely to be real. [NOTE: if you know anything about this, please contact an editor.]

Name:
Very Wild Night
[prod. WondaGurl & xxman(*)]
Notes: Instrumental leaked by KTT user xxman on July 11, 2013; finished version of the "God Level" beat. Unconfirmed as it's a strange case; xxman did the production himself, but he is known to have worked with Travis to some (very minor) extent. xxman claimed that both L$ and Travis himself confirmed that it's real, but the proof is lost. Pharaohxx also supposedly said that it was real, but this is according to Carlos who was quoting an email from him. It could simply be an idea that Travis liked and never used, or xxman may have overstated its importance.

[prod. Mano, Kanye West, Big Sean & Travis Scott]
(AKA: I Am)

The song was also mentioned by Pusha in a interview, where he said he was waiting for Kanye to finish his verse (meaning this version possibly has a mumble/unfinished verse). Pusha didn't mention The Weeknd as a feature, although he confirmed he had recorded previously; however, Pharaohxx later mentioned Abel as one of the features still, so his presence is unclear. Big Sean's name is also missing from Pharaohxx's comment. While Pharaohxx was a reliable source, Travis contributions remain unconfirmed until more evidence surfaces.

Name:
Untitled Album [Project | V2]
(AKA: Fuego, Rodeo)
Notes: During an interview on HOT97 on July 16, 2013, Travis officially announced that he was working on an album, set to be released in 2014. At the time of the announcement, Travis said that he had recently been working with Crada, so any songs made with him are likely in contention. Pharaohxx also said that the Rick Rubin rework of "Naked" would make the album. On October 31, 2013, a Rolling Stone article said that Travis was considering 'Fuego' as a title, but 'Rodeo' was eventually settled on (in March 2014 at the latest). The project continued to evolve until eventually all songs from this time had been cut.

Name: 🥇 All Around the World
Notes: Previewed once on Snapchat by Travis in a car in June 2015; sounds rough, but Travis sings over the top of most of it so it's quite hard to tell.

Name:
🏆 Antidote [Demo]
[prod. WondaGurl & Eestbound]
Notes: Early version with a rough take and different adlibs. Recorded on June 18, 2015, before being teased in a video with Metro Boomin. Performed at JYMBLYA a day or two later and then played backstage, before being re-recorded with the second and third verses being added. Travis noted in his 'Clique' interview that he did the hook and first verse in one take; there could be rough vocals where the live snippets were cut.
Link(s):
JYMBLYA Performance (Pillowcase)

Metro Snippet (Pillowcase)

Backstage JYMBLYA Snippet (Pillowcase)

Name:
Apple Pie [Demo]
[prod. Terrace Martin & 1500 or Nothin']
Notes: Mumble version recorded in May 2015. Teased briefly by Chase B on June 7, 2015. The production consists of the original instruments, probably before Travis and Mike Dean added to it. Was sent to WondaGurl for ideas.

Name:
Bruh (Ooh Na Na)
(feat. Young Thug) 
[prod. Metro Boomin & Wheezy]
Notes: Unfinished scrap that appears on the April 2015 tracklist; made in February 2015 judging by (now-deleted) Instagram snippets from Wheezy (who co-produced it with Metro Boomin). Leaked December 30, 2015.

Name:
⭐ Dinosaurus 
(feat. Yung Mazi)
Notes:
OG Filename [?]: Travis Scott - Dinosaurus_03
One of a few songs played at a Chicago meet-and-greet in March 2015. Quite rough with some mumbling. Leaked as part of a four-pack on May 16, 2016; Travis tweeted a YouTube link to it after Mazi's death.

Name:

[prod. Travis Scott]
Notes: LQ reference recorded by Travis through his phone.

Name:

Name:
Dipped [V3]
[prod. Travis Scott]
Notes: Unfinished throwaway from early 2015, unrelated to "Looove". Said to be produced by Travis but seems unlikely. Snippet leaked April 26, 2024; another leaked May 11. A portion of the intro leaked May 14. Finally leaked on July 30, 2025.

Name:
⭐ Dis Side [Demo]
[prod. Ging]
(AKA: This Side)
Notes:
OG Filename: Travis- This SIde Ref
Early solo version, dated April 22, 2015, featuring some reference vocals, an altered instrumental, and a different outro. Leaked June 13, 2019. Also leaked in acapella.

Name:
Don't (Remix)
[prod. Epikh Pro]
Notes: Freestyle over the instrumental to Bryson Tiller's "Don't". Made in 2015, around the same 'Joyride' sessions that produced "All Night", "Fantom", and "Hot Sauce". Snippet leaked April 5, 2022. Wasn't bounced; the snippet simply contains raw vocals laid over the instrumental.

Name:
Drunk [Demo 1]
(feat. Young Thug) 
[prod. Allen Ritter]
Notes:
OG Filename: Young Thug x Travis Scott x Ritter - Drunk_01
Early version dated February 24, 2015, that features different autotune from Travis, as well as extra demo vocals in place of Bieber's verse. Mentioned by Travis in a tweet on February 27, before being previewed the day after and performed live a few times. Leaked June 12, 2015. Originally intended to be released as a loosie based on info from insider skr8up, before being kept for 'Rodeo' after "Maria" was added; Reed Bennett (graphic designer who made cover arts for Travis in the past (e.g. "Blocka La Flame") and gave info on KTT in 2012-13) tweeted what seemed to be a cover art he was working on before the song was previewed.

Name:
Drunk [Demo 2]
(ref. Justin Bieber; feat. Young Thug) 
[prod. Allen Ritter]
Notes: Version previewed by Justin Bieber in his Clique interview. Transitions differently into Bieber's verse which lacks Thug's adlibs, making Bieber's adlibs sound more prominent. Likely just Bieber's verse and Thug's hook with nothing else bounced (including "Maria").

Name:
Fireman
(with Chief Keef)
Notes:
OG Filename: Travis_Chief Keef_Fireman beat
Song recorded on April 14, 2015, the same night as "Lil' Nigga" and Keef's "Nightcrawler" verse; possibly recorded over the instrumental for Lil Wayne's "Fireman", though it's more likely to be a sample.

Name:
Flying High [Demo]
(feat. Pharrell Williams) 
[prod. Pharrell Williams]
Notes: Travis had a version of the track with Pharrell's hook for several months, until Toro y Moi got on it a month or two before release; also has an unfinished second verse.

Name:
Grant To You
[prod. Allen Ritter & Travis Scott]
Notes: Recorded in mid-April 2015, and was in consideration for 'Rodeo' up until mid-July 2015.

Name:
Hold That Heat [Demo 1]
[prod. Southside]
Notes: In an interview with Lyrical Lemonade, Southside explained that the song originally originated during sessions for "one of the 'Rodeo' albums", though all Travis recorded was "adlibs and cadences". Southside also said that the song was lost for some time until Travis recovered it from an old hard drive.

Name:
I Can Tell [Demo 1]
[prod. Allen Ritter & FKi 1st]
Notes: Mumble reference version; vocals were used to create a fake "Pressure" snippet. Features an extra verse and the same hooks as the final version; Travis freestyled them in this demo and kept them. This and all later demos also feature a tweaked instrumental with additional elements.

Name:
I Can Tell [Demo 2]
[prod. Allen Ritter & FKi 1st]
Notes: Completed version with the same vocal take as the final version, but uses different vocal effects; still features the alternate production. Previewed on Snapchat alongside "Maria" on April 22, 2015.

Name:
I Can Tell [Demo 3]
[prod. Allen Ritter & FKi 1st]
Notes:
OG Filename: Travis Scott - I Can Tell_05
Effectively identical to the previous version, but with minor changes. Leaked September 3, 2019.

Name:
Joyride [Demo 1]
[prod. Hit-Boy]
Notes: Long solo reference by Travis over the first part of the instrumental. Recorded on February 26, 2015, two months before Tinashe touched the song; comments from her in an interview with Billboard suggest that it wasn't originally intended for her. Leaked July 29, 2024.

Name:
Joyride [Demo 2]
[prod. Hit-Boy]
Notes: Condensed version of the previous demo, creating a bit of a structure. Leaked July 29, 2024.

Name:
Joyride [Demo 3]
[prod. Hit-Boy]
Notes: The lead vocals from Demo 4 were recorded on February 26, 2015, in one take, over the original instrumental.

Name:
✨ Joyride [Demo 4]
[prod. Hit-Boy & Allen Ritter]
Notes: 'Completed' solo version, later shown to Tinashe; features a new verse over the beat-switch, as well as Travis performing both of Tinashe's verses and the hook. Leaked July 29, 2024.

Name:
Tinashe - Joyride [Demo 5] 
(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. Hit-Boy & Allen Ritter]
Notes: Essentially complete version of the song with two Tinashe verses; the final release is pretty much the same thing, minus Travis's parts. Tinashe's extra vocals were recorded in April 2015, but it's unclear exactly when this version was made; the filename for the following demo could imply that they were bounced at the same time. Likely the version that Tinashe intended to drop before losing the song to Rihanna. Leaked September 7, 2016.

Name:
Joyride [Demo 6]
(feat. Tinashe)
[prod. Hit-Boy & Allen Ritter]
Notes:
OG Filename: TRAVIS AND TINASHE - JOYRIDE MIX 1 ALT FIRST VERSE
Pretty much identical to the previous version, but Travis now performs the first verse (taken from previous demos). It seems that both artists at this point believed that the song belonged to them; Tinashe noted in an interview with Billboard that she had told Travis she wanted it to be the title track for her album, but Travis clearly thought it was his as he wanted a new hook for it, and ultimately sent it to Rihanna a couple of months later (see Demo 7). Leaked December 9, 2019.

Name:
Joyride [Demo 7]
(feat. Tinashe)
[prod. Hit-Boy & Allen Ritter]
Notes:
OG Filename*: Joyride To Send
Version sent to Rihanna. Identical to the previous demo, but the outro instrumental comes back in at the end after a few seconds of silence. A bounce leaked October 9, 2023.

Name:
✨ Looove [Demo]
[prod. Pharrell Williams & WondaGurl]
(AKA: Love)
Notes:
OG Filename: LOVE rough
Early version likely recorded not long after the release of 'DBR'. A LQ snippet was posted to the Tumblr page vlonexx in early September 2014, but it's not known where it originated (possibly SnapChat). Seemingly had to be AI isolated for the 'Utopia' version as the session was likely lost; WondaGurl had to send Travis the song in 2021, which is why it's thought that she may have produced on it. Leaked December 25, 2022.

Name:
Man of My City [Demo]
(feat. K CAMP) 
[prod. Metro Boomin & Southside]
(AKA: Carnage, Man in My City)
Notes: Unfinished throwaway considered for 'Rodeo' at an early stage. One of a few songs played at a Chicago meet-and-greet in March 2015, but only a snippet of the instrumental was recorded. Later finished with French Montana and Big Sean (and given to the former) during late-stage 'Rodeo' sessions and became "Man of My City". Leaked as "Man in My City"; the name "Carnage" comes from KTT insider skr8up. Rumours of a Kanye West version/feature are almost certainly false due to the timeline. Leaked October 8, 2019.

Name:

(feat. James Fauntleroy)
[prod. Boi-1da & Travis Scott]
(AKA: 411, 713 / 411, 911 / 411 (My Ex Best Friend))
Notes:
OG Filename: Travis Scott - 411
Can be seen crossed out of a late-stage tracklist for 'Rodeo'; it was probably cut due to the way that the beat was made, which made it near-impossible to get the multitracks for mixing (Travis chopped up a bounce of the original instrumental to create it). Originally titled "411", with a few other titles being in contention (see 'Tracklists'), before "My Ex Best Friend" was presumably settled on. Travis posted a snippet on Snapchat on June 13, 2015. Sold by Music Mafia and leaked November 2, 2018.

Name:
New Idea 1
[prod. Metro Boomin, Allen Ritter & Travis Scott]
Notes:
OG Filename*: METRO_ALLEN_TRAVIS NEW IDEA 1
Throwaway idea from the same session as "Wine and Hennessy", produced by Metro Boomin and Allen Ritter. Referred to as "Marietta" in the community but never actually given an official title, with the session files being titled "METRO_ALLEN_TRAVIS NEW IDEA 1". The full bounce is an hour long, and contains three separate freestyles from Travis; time inbetween these freestyles is spent working on the instrumental. The third freestyle from the session leaked June 28, 2019. The full bounce leaked May 26, 2024. Two files are linked; one file is muted in places and is technically the correct bounce, while the other is unmuted, revealing extra freestyle vocals. The unmuted version features Travis and Metro talking about Justin Bieber recording for "Drunk", Metro talking about working with 21 Savage, and Travis criticising the sequencing of Meek Mill's 'Dreams Worth More Than Money', among many other things.

[prod. Allen Ritter]

Later version utilising Gizzle's reference for the first half, though the second half has an unfinished verse and open verse. Leaked May 10, 2023, without most of the intended vocal effects; a near-completely accurate bounce leaked August 30, 2023.

[prod. Allen Ritter]
(AKA: Poet Parade)
Notes: Finished version that appears on the April 2015 tracklist; was scrapped shortly after, before being revisited and reconsidered for 'Rodeo' in July 2015. There's a good chance that this is the erased song from the July whiteboard tracklist, but it's not known why it was scrapped.

[prod. Sonny Digital & Metro Boomin]

Later version, dated June 1, 2015, with just the hook and first verse. Leaked June 13, 2019.

[prod. Sonny Digital & Metro Boomin]

[prod. Sonny Digital & Metro Boomin]
Notes:
OG Filename*: Travis Scott - Pornography_06_FT Z RO
Travis decided to use just two of Z-Ro's bars, looping and screwing them down. A bounce leaked October 9, 2023, but Z-Ro's part has minimal effects as the engineers didn't get around to adding any before he was cut.

[prod. Illangelo, The Weeknd & Ben Billions]
Notes: Lean mentioned in an interview posted on December 12, 2014, that he had sent some vocals to Travis to turn into a verse for a song with The Weeknd on it, presumably "Pray 4 Love".

[prod. Metro Boomin]

OG Filename [?]: TRAVIS SCOTT - Suicidal (BB Ref)
Song that can be seen in a list of files belonging to Metro, from the leaked footage recorded by Chris Skylark. "BB" in the filename likely refers to Bibi Bourelly; it's not known if Travis recorded. Likely recorded in January 2015 alongside Bibi's other reference tracks for "90210" and "Poet".

(AKA: Drown)

(AKA: Drown)
Notes: More complete solo version, with clear hooks and adlibs. Referred to as the "Main Ref". Uses the first verse and hook from the previous version, with re-recorded vocals based on the rest of it.

Name:
Up To Something [Demo 4]
(feat. Young Thug)
[prod. Metro Boomin, Allen Ritter & Southside]
(AKA: Drown)
Notes: First version of the song leaked; surfaced on November 24, 2015, as "Long Dicks", with the title later being confirmed as "Drown". Only has Young Thug's hooks, with Travis harmonising under the second one. Appears to have different effects on Thug's vocals, and he's missing some background vocals.

Name:

(feat. Young Thug)
[prod. Metro Boomin, Allen Ritter & Southside]
(AKA: Drown)
Notes:
OG Filename: TRVS SCTT x YNG THG - DROWN - RUFF _01
Test bounce, featuring a short portion of Thug's hook. Has a rough mix. Leaked November 19, 2023.

(feat. Young Thug)
[prod. Metro Boomin, Allen Ritter & Southside]
(AKA: Drown)
Notes:
OG Filename: TRVS SCTT x YNG THG - DROWN - RUFF _02
From the same day as the previous version, with Thug's verse now included; Travis's only contribution is harmonising, though it's more prevalent across the track than previous demos. Leaked September 27, 2022.

Better mix than the previous version, and Thug now says "ayy" on the hook. Leaked September 27, 2022.

[prod. Metro Boomin, Allen Ritter & Southside]
(AKA: Act 1)
Notes: Version with a verse from Yung Mazi; the song was retitled "Act 1" by this point. His entire verse leaked sometime in 2020, though it has none of the intended vocal effects.

Name:

(feat. Young Thug & Yung Mazi)
[prod. Metro Boomin, Allen Ritter & Southside]
(AKA: Act 1 (Drown))
Notes: Near-'complete' version of the track; appears on the April 2015 tracklist as "Act 1 (Drown)". Features new vocals from Travis. Two snippets leaked on October 8 and October 12, 2020, respectively.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 1]
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes: Earliest known version recorded on December 15, 2014, featuring Travis laying down a rough verse. An edited version leaked on August 30, 2019; the full thing leaked August 22, 2021. A better bounce leaked August 5, 2024, though Travis might be slightly off-beat.

Name:
⭐ Wasted [Demo 2] 
(feat. Yung Lean)
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes:
OG Filename: TRVS SCTT x YNG LN - WSTD - RUFF _06
'Complete' version featuring Yung Lean. Recorded in LA on December 16, 2014, though this file/version was bounced on February 16, 2015. Features a verse and pre-hook from Yung Lean, as well as a hook and verse from Travis that are different from the final version. Also has a slightly different instrumental to later versions. Auctioned by Music Mafia and leaked on December 27, 2017.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 3] 
(feat. Yung Lean)
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes: A rough, unbounced version recorded on May 29, 2015, featuring a mumble verse and Lean's pre-hook; Travis's hook was presumably scrapped at this point, and possibly also Lean's verse. An edited version leaked on August 30, 2019; the full thing leaked August 22, 2021. A better bounce leaked May 29, 2024.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 4]
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes: Another solo reference, featuring the intro from the final and a mumble version of the final verse. Also recorded on May 29, 2015. A bounce leaked July 18, 2024.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 5]
(ref. Price)
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes: Reference for Travis, performed by Price of Audio Push; he's credited as a writer under the name "Larry Price". Directly based on the flow from the previous demo. A bounce leaked July 18, 2024.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 6]
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes:
OG Filename: Travis Scott_Wasted_BPM 67.5_KEY B-Minor
Version featuring just Travis's intro and verse from the final version, minus some vocal effects. Leaked July 11, 2022. Young Thug was briefly considered as a feature around this time.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 7]
(feat. Juicy J)
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes:
OG Filename: Travis Scott_Wasted_BPM 67.5 Juicy Verse rev2
First known version with Juicy J; he has a different verse from later versions, but this verse was actually recorded after the other one (hence "rev(ision)2" in the filename). Leaked December 24, 2019.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 8]
(feat. Juicy J)
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes:
OG Filename: Travis Scott - WASTED_09
Version dated June 19, 2015, that's pretty close to the final release, but lacks the Pimp C. sample, instead using Travis's "wasted or nah" intro as a refrain of sorts. Leaked July 11, 2022.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 9] 
(feat. Juicy J)
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes:
OG Filename*: TRAVIS SCOTT_WASTED_JUICY J_10_for mix
Pre-mix demo that is almost identical to the final release, just less polished. Also has a strange piece of additional production near the end. Pimp C.'s sample was supposed to be included on this version, but it was missing from the files for some reason; it could be that the open sections were added in preparation, before they had access to Pimp C.'s vocals. A bounce leaked October 9, 2023. A better bounce leaked July 18, 2024.

Name:
Wasted [Demo 10] 
(feat. King Krule & Juicy J)
[prod. Metro Boomin & Ging]
Notes: Version featuring King Krule; appeared on a late-stage tracklist for 'Rodeo'. Likely conceived and scrapped very late, as the song was near completion with Juicy J and the Pimp C. sample before Krule touched the song, and Travis confirmed to a fan sometime around July 3, 2015, that Krule was on the album. It's likely that Juicy is still on this version, with Travis wanting Krule to do a hook that was scrapped in favour of Pimp C. for whatever reason, but this is pure speculation. It's also possible that nothing was ever recorded by Krule.

Name:
What's Next 
(feat. Quentin Miller)
Notes: Supposedly recorded in 2014 (likely post-'DBR'), but has never been confirmed. Unfinished, with Travis mumbling in some places; speculation that Quentin's verse is a reference for Drake is likely untrue based on the lyrics. Originally leaked September 10, 2016; leaked in higher quality on February 3, 2020.

Name:
Whole Lots Changed [Demo 1]
(feat. Yung Mazi)
[prod. Hit-Boy]
Notes: Appears on the April 2015 tracklist; the notes seem to suggest that they were considering cutting Mazi's part to make room for a third verse.

Name:
Whole Lots Changed [Demo 2]
[prod. Hit-Boy]
Notes: Mazi was seemingly cut from the song shortly after; his name doesn't appear as a feature on the April 23, 2015 considerations sheet (see 'Tracklists').

Name:
Whole Lots Changed [Demo 3]
(feat. Fredo Santana)
[prod. Hit-Boy]
Notes: Fredo got on the song in May 2015; it was then in consideration for 'Rodeo' until mid-July 2015. Probably nearly identical to the released version.

Name:
Wine and Hennessy
[prod. Metro Boomin & Allen Ritter]
Notes:
OG Filename*: WINE AND HENNESY IDEA
Features Travis laying down reference vocals for about ten minutes over an evolving beat produced by Metro Boomin and Allen Ritter. Privately sold by Music Mafia and then previewed via an edited snippet under the name "All The Time"; it was labelled as featuring The Weeknd, but this was an intentional false claim to catch scammers. Sometimes shortened to "Hennessy" before the title was confirmed as "WINE AND HENNESY IDEA" (with the misspelling). The song wasn't bounced, and Music Mafia didn't even attempt it, so it leaked without the vocal effects that it was intended to have on February 6, 2018. An inaccurate recreation surfaced in November 2020. A completely accurate bounce leaked September 7, 2022.

Name:
[Multiple Unknown]
[prod. Kyor Gyoti]
Notes: Travis and Kyor made more songs than just "Oh No*" across 2014-2015. No other information is available.

Name:
[Multiple Unknown]
(with Young Thug)
Notes: Producer TM88 noted in an interview with Zane Lowe that Travis and Thug recorded a lot of music when they were touring together in early 2015. "Bruh (Ooh Na Na)", "Drunk", and "Up To Something" were all recorded prior to the tour, so we don't have any real idea of what they made.

(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. Diplo]

More complete version, dated September 22, 2014, with rough autotune, some different adlibs, the tweaked instrumental from previous versions, and no outro. Previewed at a club by Metro in late October 2014, and multiple times in early 2015. Leaked April 15, 2019.

(feat. Travis Scott)

The title "First Quarter '15" came from a misunderstanding in a KTT thread collating all of GOODMUSICINSIDER's claims; he never actually called it his favourite song off of 'Rodeo', but was instead referencing when 'Rodeo' was supposed to drop.

(with Chedda Da Connect)
[prod. FredOnEm]

Name:
Fuego
[prod. Metro Boomin & G Koop]
Notes: In October 2014, a snippet of Metro and Travis listening to a beat surfaced. This beat would later become the intro to Pusha T's 'King Push – Darkest Before Dawn: The Prelude', but it was originally intended for Travis, titled "Fuego". It's thought, however, that Travis didn't record any vocals.

Name:
Kanye West - Grave
(feat. Travis Scott)
Notes: Appears on The Weeknd's playlist in a Beats 1 collaborative commercial debuted at the MTV VMAs on August 30, 2015. Realistically, this makes no sense; the Kanye tracker previously speculated that this was the last version before it was given to Travis, but he clearly had the song long before then. Even if we assume it was made much earlier, it's still much longer than any other version, which only adds to the confusion; Kanye also didn't finish his vocals until very late in the 'Rodeo' sessions, so it probably can't be from earlier. A censored snippet can be heard in the commercial, but it essentially sounds like the released version.

Migo Gang 
(with OG Maco & Migos)

[prod. Illangelo, The Weeknd & Ben Billions]
Notes: It's possible that a verse was made out of Lean's vocals from Demo 2 before he was cut, but it's unconfirmed. It's still unclear if "Pray 4 Love" is even the song that Lean was referring to, but it's the best guess currently.

Name: Quintana, Pt. 3
Notes: Referenced by KTT insider skr8up in early 2015 as being on 'Rodeo'. Though skr8up was proven to be a part of Travis's crew at the time and some of the info he gave turned out to be true, we've seen no further evidence of the existence of a Pt. 3, so it could easily have been a joke. Having said that, Travis doesn't say "quintana" at all during Pt. 2, so it could be a song that we know about with its original title.

Name:
Birds In The Trap Sing McKnight
(2015 - 2016)
Notes: Era begins after the release of 'Rodeo', and ends when the project was released on September 2, 2016. Seen by Travis as a stepping-stone between 'Rodeo' and 'Astroworld', the latter of which he announced before this project had even been released. Tentatively titled 'Tina' for a little while.

Name:
A-Team [Demo]
[prod. Allen Ritter]
(AKA: A Team)
Notes:
OG Filename: A Team_New RUff
Near-dentical to the final release, with a rougher mix. Leaked September 27, 2022.

Name:
All I Know [Demo]
(feat. Kamaiyah)
[prod. Mustard]
(AKA: All I Know Is This)
Notes: Leftover from 'Birds' according to a tentative tracklist; it was indirectly mentioned in an early July, 2016 interview by Kamaiyah. Seemingly scrapped very late as Travis said on July 18 and August 12 that Kamaiyah was meant to be on the album. Sold by Music Mafia and leaked October 8, 2018; Kamaiyah then dropped a different version (new verse and no Mustard tag) two days later in response to the leak.

Name:
Beibs In the Trap [Demo]
(feat. NAV)
[prod. NAV]
(AKA: She Wants More)
Notes: Version teased on June 22, 2016, the day that Travis recorded on it. Features an early mix, and may feature NAV's early take unless he had already re-recorded his verse. Titled "She Wants More" at the time.

Name: Easy [V1]
Notes: Very rough version previewed sometime in late 2015 (December 30 is the furthest I can trace it (and that source is now gone), but it was most likely posted a few days earlier).

Name:
✨ Easy [V2] 
(ref. / feat. Rook Monroe)
Notes: Previewed by James Harden on January 16, 2017, and Travis later played it on Snapchat in May 2017, though it was likely recorded around 'ANTI' sessions sometime in late 2015 to early 2016 based on the Rook Monroe feature. Multiple high-quality snippets leaked over the course of 2017, until an edited version with an extra chorus (~3:20) was eventually leaked by Music Mafia on December 25, 2017 (the real, unedited version (~2:49) has since been made available). Rook Monroe is primarily a writer and was possibly laying down a reference verse. Possibly one of the first 2016 'Astroworld' considerations.

Name:
Ecstasy
(with The-Dream)
Notes:
OG Filename [?]: ECSTACY super ruff
Rough demo; The-Dream said on Instagram that he forgot that it even existed so it could be very old, but it sounds post-'Rodeo'; it was likely made around the same 'ANTI' sessions that produced "Woo", but it doesn't appear to be a reference for Rihanna. Privately sold by Music Mafia and leaked February 7, 2018.

Rough one-take throwaway that features production from Gezin of 808 Mafia. Previewed once in a club in February 2016 alongside "RaRa" and "Left Cheek Right Cheek". It was the first Travis song ever publicly auctioned by Music Mafia, in June 2017; it leaked just over a year later on July 28, 2018.

[prod. WondaGurl]

OG Filename: Hot Sauce Ruff
Alternate version made in the same February 2016 session as "Regular", presumably after taking the song back from Tinashe. Sounds rougher than 'V1', but is likely to be a newer version. Leaked June 20, 2019. The instrumental was eventually reworked and given to Chilly Chills for "GOAT".

Name:

(feat. Quentin Miller; add. Jeremih)
[prod. Vinylz & Allen Ritter]
(AKA: LCRC)
Notes: Throwaway that was worked on for several years. First previewed in November 2015, it later got played alongside "Forgive Me" and "RaRa" in a club in February 2016. The track was temporarily given to DJ SpinKing and reworked, before Travis took it back and performed it live for the final time in January 2017. Vinylz later said in 2018 that he planned to "finish" it (see 'V2'). Has a short bridge from Jeremih, who may have originally had his own version of the track before Travis created this one (see "Try Me"). Leaked October 6, 2019.

Name:
LoFi [V1]
(with Quavo)
[prod. Murda Beatz & Cubeatz]
Notes: Offset is known to have been added to the song around August 2016, so a version without him exists, likely made sometime in mid-2016.

Lose [Demo 1]
(add. Cassie)
[prod. Vinylz, Ging & Allen Ritter]
Notes: Early version that leaked shortly before the album was released, but was later finished and included on it anyway. Features a more bare-bones instrumental and some rough vocals. Performed live in June 2016; leaked August 15, 2016.

Name:
Lose [Demo 2]
(add. Cassie)
[prod. Vinylz, Ging, Allen Ritter & MIKE DEAN]
Notes:
OG Filename: Lose mike dean add rough mix

Name:
Money Power Respect [V2]
(feat. Meek Mill)
[prod. Vinylz & Allen Ritter]
(AKA: M.P.R.)

Name:
Myles [V1]
[prod. Myles William & Cubeatz]
Notes: Rough throwaway named after its producer, Myles William. Teased in the studio with 21 Savage in late August 2016, but it sounds like the vocals are being recorded and may not have been kept for any bounce.

Name:
Myles [V2]
[prod. Myles William & Cubeatz]
Notes: Very rough; doesn't share any vocals with the 'V1' snippet. Leaked September 7, 2016.

Name: 🏆 Nine One One

Was supposed to debut on .WAV Radio, but it's unknown what became of it.

OG Filename*: trav uzi pressure
Kanye throwaway that was worked on in 2014-15, originally starting out as "Get Off Me"; this final known version features Travis and was made during 'SWISH' sessions (see the Kanye tracker for information on other versions). Another version had a snippet leak through Music Mafia in mid-2017. This version was teased on Instagram Live by Che Pope, on October 22, 2020. The intro samples the 2012 film 'Silver Linings Playbook', while the synths are sampled from "Haunted", from the 'Maniac' soundtrack. Uzi was present in the studio, but didn't record for this song. Leaked May 26, 2024. The first released version was identical to this leak.

Name:

(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. Low Pros & Menace]
Notes: Solo version previewed by Menace on November 15, 2017; snippets are lost. Exact year is unclear but it was likely Kanye's song at this point. A fake version was leaked January 30, 2018, by a suspected Logan Smitherman (RIP) alt. He said via DM that it was produced by Mike Dean, Menace, Diplo, and Charlie Heat; he also stated that it started out as a Travis x Diplo song, which is definitely false. Seems to be real to some degree, as it has a realistic beat similar to Demo 1 and has the full hook vocals without sounding like it was a DIY acapella, but the quality and cuts make it sound fake, and he was known to have the stems/potentially the project files. He also claimed to have "cleaned it up", so, even if this is real, it's still edited.

Some of Kanye's lyrics near the beginning were reused on "Cudi Montage" from 'KIDS SEE GHOSTS', and most of his second verse was used (and tweaked) by Travis on the final version. The song was supposedly intended for Kanye's 'Love Everyone' at some stage in Spring 2018, though a scene from 'Look Mom I Can Fly' shows Kevin Parker playing the instrumental for Travis (see 'Demo 1'); how the song came together is unknown, but Travis seemingly showed it to Kanye and took it back after he didn't use it. Name: Dystopia [Project]
Notes: Mixtape that was supposedly set to drop in December 2021, with 'Utopia' following in early 2022, but its release was cancelled after the Astroworld Festival tragedy. The information comes from a producer closely affiliated with Cactus Jack that claimed to have production on both projects, though he wasn't on 'Utopia' in the end. It's possible that songs like "Heartbeat" were planned for 'Dystopia', but this is pure speculation. See the relevant entry on the 'Tracklists' sheet for more information.

Name:
Utopia [May 14, 2020]
(ref. Kanye West)
Notes: Seemingly rough copy of 'Utopia' dated May 14, 2020, featuring Kanye West performing freestyles over some beats, likely meant as a reference; no Travis vocals. Ye was rumoured to be executive producing the album in 2020, and it appears that this is from that time. Features 15 tracks (with some having two versions each), including early versions of "Delresto" and "Big Champ". Besides the latter, no other title is known. Individual files can be found in the main unreleased section.

Name:
Juice WRLD - KTM Drip [Demo]
(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. DT & Nick Mira]
Notes: A deleted tweet from Lil Bibby in 2021 confirmed that Travis was intended to appear on an early version of the posthumous Juice WRLD album 'The Party Never Ends'. Reliable sources have since confirmed that the songs Travis recorded for were "KTM Drip" and "Purple Substance", and that his verses ended up being scrapped due to Juice's manager Pete disliking them. Travis Scott was added after a fake tracklist, with him being added to "KTM Drip", went viral.

Name:

(feat. Travis Scott)
[prod. Chris Handsome & Rex Kudo]
Notes: A deleted tweet from Lil Bibby in 2021 confirmed that Travis was intended to appear on an early version of the posthumous Juice WRLD album 'The Party Never Ends'. Reliable sources have since confirmed that the songs Travis recorded for were "KTM Drip" and "Purple Substance", and that his verses ended up being scrapped due to Juice's manager Pete disliking the verses. Another attempt to release the song occured, when it was picked as one of the non-fan-chosen songs for the deluxe of 'TPNE' in early 2025, but it never ended up dropping.

Name:

(2020)
Notes: Collaborative project with Kid Cudi that was effectively announced on April 23, 2020, when the title track was released. Other songs from the project are known about, but the album never materialised. Cudi has made multiple tweets over the years addressing the status of the project, but it is currently, as of August 2024, not planned for release (see Sources).

However this might've been disproven by Rob49 in a interview with RapTV, where he said Travis played the song with "The Weeknd and Bad Bunny" for him and asked to hop on the track (which he passed. Name:
Meltdown [Demo 1]
[prod. BNYX]
Notes: In a 2025 GQ Cover, Pusha T recalled Travis playing 'Utopia' for Pharrell (while he was also seemingly present) in May 2023 in Paris. Pusha claimed that Travis hid Drake's part off of "Meltdown" (which is mostly a diss towards Pharrell) when playing the songs. However, it can be verified that Drake hadn't recorded anything for the song yet (he only recorded in July); this was also confirmed by Travis in a 2026 Rolling Stone interview. This possibly confirms a Travis solo demo; it's also worth considering though that the song might've never be played in that occasion, with Pusha either making it up (for the sake of the "beef" with Travis), or he could've just be misremembering; matter of fact, based on other loosy infos, the song possibly didn't even exist at that time and only started days before the album released (although that is still unconfirmed too). And even more, some Travis' lyrics on the song directly reference Drake's, so the situation is not very clear.

Name:
Post-Utopia
(2023 - Present)
Notes:
Era begins after the release of 'Utopia'. Will be redesigned later.
In a January 2026 interview Travis confirmed a new project is being worked on, saying he's putting his "body and soul" in it and compared it to "a level of what 'Rodeo' could sound on the ultra scale stadium life".
A new album was teased as far back as early 2024, when Travis confirmed he was recording one while on the Circus Maximus Tour. He had also suggested that the song titles would've been related to the cities they were recorded in; this idea, however is now very likely scrapped.
